summary: Matched sysdolphin/baselib/psdisptev.c by replacing the nonmatching psSetupTev stub with a full GX TEV setup implementation, cleaning up psdisptev.h prototypes and include guards, and marking psdisptev.c as Matching in configure.py with the required -Cpp_exceptions on flag. The same configure change also marks hsd_3B33.c as Matching with that flag, but the provided diff evidence does not show source changes for hsd_3B33.c.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for sysdolphin/baselib/psdisptev.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ..., extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"]).
Changed configure.py entry for sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B33.c from NonMatching to Matching with extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"], though no hsd_3B33.c diff is present in the slice.
Replaced the psSetupTev placeholder comment with a full implementation taking u32* arg0 and switching on arg0[1] masked by 0x80100480.
Added prevTev caching logic: psSetupTev returns early when the masked TEV state equals prevTev[0], and psSetupTevInvalidState invalidates the cached state by setting prevTev[0] to -1.
Changed prevTev from static s32 prevTev[2] with an address comment to static u32 prevTev[2].

Converted psSetupTevCommon GX calls from numeric literals to named GX constants such as GX_TEVSTAGE0, GX_TEXCOORD0, GX_TEXMAP0, GX_COLOR0A0, GX_TEV_ADD, GX_TB_ZERO, GX_CS_SCALE_1, GX_TEVPREV, and GX_TEV_SWAP0.

Implemented multiple TEV state cases in psSetupTev, configuring GXSetNumTevStages, GXSetNumTexGens, GXSetTevOrder, GXSetTevColorIn, GXSetTevAlphaIn, and GXSetTevOp based on bitmask values such as 0x80000000, 0x400, 0x100400, 0x80100480, and related fallthrough cases.
Updated psdisptev.c to include its own header, psdisptev.h, instead of directly including platform.h.
Renamed the psdisptev.h include guard from the address-style GALE01_3A3920 to SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_PSDISPTEV_H.
Removed placeholder.h from psdisptev.h and replaced the psSetupTev(UNK_T) declaration with psSetupTev(u32*).
Removed address comments from psdisptev.h function declarations.
smart_moves: Used named Dolphin GX enum constants in existing and new GX calls, making the TEV configuration legible while preserving the same numeric API values needed for matching.
Kept intentional switch fallthroughs for cases that clear bit 0x80 before sharing the lower-bit TEV setup path, matching the source control flow implied by the diff.
Modeled TEV state caching with prevTev so repeated psSetupTev calls skip redundant GX state setup when the relevant masked bits have not changed.
Narrowed the public psSetupTev prototype from unknown placeholder type to u32*, which aligns with the implementation indexing arg0[1].
Included psdisptev.h from psdisptev.c so the implementation is checked against its public prototype.
Used the build-system Matching flag and the special -Cpp_exceptions on compiler option together, documenting that this object needs non-default compiler behavior to match.
decomp_lessons: For GX-heavy code, replacing raw integer arguments with SDK enum names can improve readability without necessarily disrupting matching, as long as the enum values compile identically.

When a function reads or mutates bitfields through an opaque pointer, a minimally typed pointer such as u32* can be a useful intermediate improvement over UNK_T if the surrounding struct is not yet identified.
Switch fallthrough can be required for matching state-machine or bit-normalization code; preserve fallthrough structure when cases first clear bits and then reuse another case body.
Static state caches may need unsigned storage even when invalidated with -1; here prevTev is u32 but still stores the all-ones sentinel from psSetupTevInvalidState.
A matched source file may require explicit per-object compiler flags in configure.py; future matches should check whether -Cpp_exceptions on is needed before marking the object Matching.
Header cleanup is part of decomp progress: remove placeholder includes and address-based include guards once real prototypes and file-scoped guard names are available.
naming_conventions: Header include guards should use path-based names like SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_PSDISPTEV_H rather than generated address labels such as GALE01_3A3920.
Public functions in this file use the psSetupTev* naming family: psSetupTevCommon, psSetupTevInvalidState, and psSetupTev.
Local temporaries can keep compiler/register-oriented names such as temp_r5 when they are useful for preserving a known match shape.
GX constants should be spelled with SDK-style names such as GX_TEVSTAGE0, GX_TEXCOORD_NULL, GX_TEXMAP_NULL, GX_CC_CPREV, GX_CA_APREV, and GX_MODULATE instead of raw numeric literals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked psdisptev.c Matching only after replacing the stubbed psSetupTev body with a full switch-based implementation.
Applied extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"] to the matching object in configure.py, indicating the object requires that compiler option for a binary match.
Used a bitmask expression arg0[1] & 0x80100480 to derive the switch discriminator and cache key.
Maintained mutation of both arg0[1] and prevTev[0] in cases that clear 0x80, which likely preserves original side effects and fallthrough behavior.
Represented the TEV mode logic as explicit case labels over observed constants rather than abstracting into helper functions, which is usually safer for matching control flow and call ordering.
follow_up_queries: Check whether hsd_3B33.c was already matched in a previous commit or included in this PR through build-configuration-only status changes.
Search for other baselib files needing extra_cflags=["-Cpp_exceptions on"] before marking them Matching.
Identify the real struct type for the psSetupTev u32* argument so the arg0[1] bitfield access can eventually be replaced with a named field.
Look for other headers still using GALE01 address-style include guards or placeholder.h only for UNK_T prototypes.
